Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me analyze this order fulfillment process to construct a Log Skeleton model I need to identify the constraints based on the natural flow of activities

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Receive Order RO Marks entry point Check Availability CA Verify stock Reserve Items RI Reserve items after confirming availability Pick Items PI Collect items after reservation Quality Check QC Inspect picked items Pack Order PO Pack after quality check Generate Shipping Label GSL Create label before dispatch Dispatch D Send to carrier Invoice Customer IC Bill customer Record Payment RP Record payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before constraints CA always before RI cant reserve items before checking availability RI always before PI cant pick before reserving PI always before QC cant check quality before picking QC always before PO cant pack before quality check PO always before GSL cant generate label before packing GSL always before D cant dispatch without label D always before IC typically invoice after dispatch though sometimes before IC always before RP cant record payment before invoicing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Always After constraints RO always after nothing its the start CA always after RO must receive order to check availability RI always after CA PI always after RI QC always after PI PO always after QC GSL always after PO D always after GSL IC always after D or could be IC always after PO RP always after IC

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait let me reconsider Always After means if the first activity occurs the second should have been executed previously So this is essentially the same as Always Before but in reverse direction

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly Follows constraints These are more specific activities that should happen immediately after each other Looking at the process flow RO CA order received then immediately check availability CA RI check availability then reserve RI PI reserve then pick PI QC pick then quality check QC PO quality check then pack PO GSL pack then generate label GSL D label then dispatch D IC dispatch then invoice could be concurrent too IC RP invoice then record payment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Never Together Hard to say if any activities truly cannot co exist In some processes certain activities might be mutually exclusive paths Let me think

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Actually in this linear process there might not be strong never together constraints However this seems like a fairly linear process

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Equivalence No activities seem to occur the same number of times consistently

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences RO 1 should occur exactly once per order CA 1 exactly once per order RI 1 exactly once per order PI 1 exactly once per order QC 1 exactly once per order PO 1 exactly once per order GSL 1 exactly once per order D 1 exactly once per order IC 1 exactly once per order RP 0 1 might occur 0 or 1 times payment might not be recorded yet

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Let me refine my analysis Always Before if X then Y should have been executed previously Y always before X RI always before RI No thats not right Let me re read

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Always Before If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously So if RI occurs then CA should have occurred previously RI is the first activity in the pair CA is the second

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So RI CA always before PI RI always before QC PI always before PO QC always before GSL PO always before D GSL always before IC D always before or PO always before RP IC always before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Always After CA always after RO RI always after CA and RO PI always after RI and CA RO QC always after PI and RI CA RO PO always after QC and PI RI CA RO GSL always after PO and QC PI RI CA RO D always after GSL and PO QC PI RI CA RO IC always after D and GSL PO QC PI RI CA RO RP always after IC and D GSL PO QC PI RI CA RO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly Follows RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP
